sections.paragraph:
ἀπολακτίζω 1 aor. ἀπελάκτισα (s. λακτίζω; Theognis et al.) intr. to engage in a kicking motion, kick away, kick off, kick up, kick (M. Ant. 10, 28, 1 [of a kicking and screaming pig]; Ps.-Lucian, Asin. 18 [the ass kicks out with its hind legs as it runs off]). So in the use of Dt 32:15 in 1 Cl 3:1 (cp. Just., D. 20, 1), where the mng. in the light of the context prob. = ‘kick up (the heels)’, in a demonstration of proud independence spurn (cp. Aeschyl. Prom. 651; Plut., Ant. 36, 2 τὰ καλά; as a saying of Plato in Diog. L. 5, 2 [Aristotle ‘kicks up’ at Plato, i.e. leaves him behind in the Academy]).—DELG s.v. λάξ (adv. ‘with the foot’).

aor. aor. = aorist

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

intr. intr. = intransitive

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 115
